title: WattEV's 370 Tesla Semi Order and Expanded MCS Charging Infrastructure
claim_supported: WattEV ordered 370 Tesla Semi trucks (announced 9 May 2026), the largest single electric truck deployment in California; >300 units deployed under a joint program with the Port of Oakland; new MCS charging stations opening 2026 at Port of Oakland, Fresno, Stockton.
locator: Article body, order/timeline/Port of Oakland paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: Primary buyer/capital-deployment demand evidence for the US MCS leg of F-07; WattEV is the buyer/operator (not the connector/thermal vendor), so this is customer evidence, not vendor marketing.

india_origin_audit.notes: US EV-charging trade publication reporting on US companies (WattEV, Port of Oakland); no India connection.

title: Kempower, Windrose, and EV Realty Validate Megawatt Charging System (MCS) Interoperability Under Real World Conditions
claim_supported: Kempower, Windrose, and EV Realty conducted a successful MCS charge at EV Realty's San Bernardino truck-charging hub (announced 25 March 2026), delivering up to 1.2 MW ahead of the site's April 2026 opening, described as a concrete step toward commercial MCS availability for fleet operators.
locator: Press release body, fetched directly this session
notes: Independent second US MCS demand source; EV Realty is a truck-charging-hub developer/operator (customer-side), not the connector vendor.

india_origin_audit.notes: US truck-charging infrastructure developer (San Bernardino, CA site); no India connection.

title: Sinochem New Energy 2023-2024 Heavy-Duty Battery Swap Station Complete Equipment Framework Tender
authors_or_org: Sinochem New Energy Limited Company (中化新能源有限公司)
claim_supported: Open framework tender for complete heavy-truck battery-swap-station equipment (charging module, swap module, monitoring room, backup batteries), max RMB 2.5M/set incl. tax and software IP license; bidding deadline 8 Aug 2023; manufacturer-only (no dealer/agent bids); eligibility requires >=15 delivered battery-swap units and >=2 heavy-truck swap-station projects since 2019; max 4 suppliers selected via framework agreement.
locator: Tender notice body, eligibility/pricing/timeline sections, fetched directly this session
notes: PRIMARY demand evidence for F-07's CN binding gate: confirms CN swap-equipment procurement uses open competitive framework tenders (not CATL-exclusive sole-source), but with steep track-record eligibility bars that constrain new/foreign-entrant access absent a qualified CN manufacturing partner.

india_origin_audit.notes: Chinese state-linked chemicals/energy conglomerate procurement portal; no India connection.

title: Guangdong Tower Energy 2025-2027 60V50Ah Battery Swap Charging Cabinet Tender
authors_or_org: Guangdong Tower Energy (广东铁塔能源)
claim_supported: Open framework tender for ~4,000 battery-swap charging cabinets (31,800 chambers total, five models), RMB 40.68M incl. tax, 2-year framework period, 15-calendar-day delivery SLA, 6-year warranty; manufacturer-only, no dealer/agent bids.
locator: Tender notice body, scope/budget/eligibility sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Independent second CN procurement/tender source (different procuring entity, different vehicle class - light two-wheeler swap cabinets) confirming the open-tender pattern for swap-equipment recurs beyond a single observed instance. TIER UPGRADE (P4 curation): retiered T2->T1 -- this is a directly-fetched buyer tender/procurement notice, meeting the T1 definition of 'buyer tender/procurement/specification.'

india_origin_audit.notes: China Tower-affiliated energy subsidiary; Chinese tender-aggregation platform. No India connection.

title: CATL: Choco-Swap Cumulative Stations Reach 1,650, Over 3,000 Expected by End-2026
authors_or_org: Sina Finance (新浪财经/新浪科技)
claim_supported: CATL's Choco-Swap battery-swap stations reached 1,650 across 127 cities by May 2026 (600+ added in under 6 months), targeting >3,000 stations across ~190 cities by end-2026; 99-second swap cycle; 800V Choco-Swap 26# battery.
locator: Article body, station-count/target/swap-cycle paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: Installed-base and high-cycle-duty evidence for the CN swap-infrastructure premise underlying F-07's CN leg.

claim_supported: NIO's official press release confirms cumulative battery-swap count reached 100 million as of 22:33:18 on 6 February 2026.

authors_or_org: China Securities Journal (中国证券报, cs.com.cn)
claim_supported: NIO operated 3,839 national battery-swap stations as of the article date; completed 171,600 swaps on 1 May 2026 alone and 1,031,500 swaps over the 5-day holiday; 1,035 highway swap stations saved users 220,000+ hours of waiting time.

title: 坚持以攻克连接器行业难题为导向 瑞可达进入蓝来汽车与美国T公司产业链 (Recodeal Enters NIO and 'US T Company' Supply Chains)

claim_supported: Recodeal's (瑞可达) custom-developed battery-swap connector solution (using floating-tolerance technology) received NIO's final approval, becoming NIO's primary supplier for this critical component and an important high-voltage-connector supplier; patented environmental spring/composite terminal designs reduce contact resistance and extend insertion-removal cycles.

title: Phoenix Contact Developed a One-Megawatt CCS2 Charging Connector
claim_supported: Phoenix Contact's 1MW-class CCS2 connector: 1,000A boost/800A continuous with active water-glycol cooling (temp sensors, leak detection), 1,000V DC rated, IP67; MCS cables expected 'early 2026', MCS inlets 'medium term'; without active cooling, 800A boost/600A continuous.
locator: Article body, specifications section, fetched directly this session
notes: Critical competitor finding: Phoenix Contact already ships in-house liquid-cooled thermal engineering for MCS-class connectors in 2026, directly bearing on F-07's merchant-thermal-subsystem wedge.

india_origin_audit.notes: US EV-charging trade newsletter reporting on a German connector manufacturer. No India connection.

title: CEPC Matures, But Approval Is On Hold
claim_supported: CEPC will not be included in China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030) despite completing full Technical Design Reports (accelerator TDR 2023, detector TDR Oct 2025); IHEP study leader Wang Yifang states plans to resubmit CEPC for consideration in 2030, or join CERN's FCC and abandon CEPC if FCC is approved first.
locator: Article body, 15th Five-Year Plan / Wang Yifang quote paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: CRITICAL negative refresh of F-09's CN demand_trigger_2030_2034 (CEPC construction decision); directly contradicts the seed's assumption that CEPC construction timing lands in the early 2030s.

india_origin_audit.notes: CERN Courier is published by IOP Publishing (Bristol, UK) in association with CERN (Geneva, Switzerland); reports on IHEP (China). No India connection.

title: ITER Gyrotron Installation Advances
claim_supported: ITER's updated Baseline-2024 requires 48 gyrotrons at start of operations (double the original 24), plus 24 more for the deuterium-tritium phase; Japan and Russia were each separately contracted in 2025 for up to 20 (option for 4 more = 24) additional gyrotrons, with deliveries extending to 2032.
locator: Article body, expanded-requirements and new-procurement-contracts paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: Positive refresh/second independent US/EU timing and demand source for F-09; shows gyrotron (and window/coupler) demand is larger and extends later than the seed assumed.

india_origin_audit.notes: UK-headquartered nuclear-industry trade publication (Progressive Media International); reports on ITER (France-based international project). No India connection.

title: SiN (SN287) RF Windows | Fine Ceramics (Advanced Ceramics)
claim_supported: Kyocera sells a catalog silicon-nitride (SN287) RF window product explicitly marketed for gyrotrons and klystrons, with high-frequency performance exceeding 150GHz in the millimeter-wave domain, using Kyocera's proprietary ceramic-to-metal bonding technology.
locator: Product page body, fetched directly this session
notes: Critical competitor finding: an established, well-resourced ceramics maker already sells merchant RF windows for klystrons/gyrotrons, narrowing F-09's wedge to the diagnostics/qualification layer rather than window fabrication itself.

india_origin_audit.notes: Kyocera Corporation is headquartered in Kyoto, Japan. No India connection.

title: 骄成超声(688392)深度研报：技术平台化驱动多业务爆发，券商维持买入评级 (Jiaocheng Ultrasonic Deep Research Report: Platform Technology Drives Multi-Business Growth)
claim_supported: Jiaocheng Ultrasonic (688392.SH) remains 'bound to' (绑定) CATL and BYD as leading customers for electrode/tab-welding equipment as of late 2025; forecasts 2025 battery-equipment segment revenue of RMB 200-250M; has already deployed 2.5D/3D ultrasonic scanning microscopes for advanced-packaging QC to domestic leading customers and supplies composite-current-collector ultrasonic rolling-weld equipment to CATL.
locator: Analyst report body, customer-concentration/segment-forecast/new-product-line paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: REFRESHES atlas L16-028 (2022-vintage) per binding P4 requirement; confirms CATL/BYD buyer concentration remains current in 2025 and surfaces a new incumbent-response risk (Jiaocheng's own move into scanning/QC technology) directly relevant to F-10's wedge and F-11's competitive landscape.

institutions.name: Shanghai Jiaocheng Ultrasonic Wave Technology Co., Ltd.

india_origin_audit.notes: Major Chinese financial-data platform reporting on a Shanghai-listed (STAR Market) Chinese ultrasonic-equipment maker. No India connection.

title: Tecnar's Laser UT Industrial Applications
claim_supported: Tecnar's laser-ultrasonic industrial applications page lists six verticals (hot-metal inspection, microstructure analysis, additive manufacturing, linear-weld evaluation, thin coatings, composite/aerospace inspection) and does not list battery/busbar or pipeline applications; Tecnar's Weldspector product provides <10-second in-line weld-quality diagnosis for resistive overlap seam and laser butt welds on moving, hot targets.
locator: Applications page body, fetched directly this session
notes: Confirms the closest existing laser-ultrasonic competitor has not yet entered F-10's two target verticals (battery tab welds, pipeline girth welds), supporting the 'no-man's-land' framing while flagging Tecnar as the most likely fast-follower.

india_origin_audit.notes: Tecnar is headquartered in Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville, Quebec, Canada. No India connection.

title: PipeWIZARD iX Ultrasonic Testing System for Girth Weld Inspection
claim_supported: Evident's PipeWIZARD iX is a contact-based phased-array ultrasonic testing (PAUT)/TOFD/UT system for pipeline girth-weld inspection (onshore/offshore), validated to ASTM E-1961, API 1104, and DNV-OS-F101; probes couple directly to the pipe surface.
locator: Product page body, technology/target-market/contact-method sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Confirms the dominant contact/couplant-based incumbent baseline for pipeline girth-weld inspection that F-10's non-contact laser-UT approach would need to displace.

india_origin_audit.notes: Evident Corporation is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan (spun off from Olympus in 2022), with US operations. No India connection.

title: EWI's Laser-based Pipeline Weld Inspection System
claim_supported: EWI has developed and refined laser-based pipeline inspection 'pigs' for onshore and offshore use over 15 years; the current prototype performs laser topography scanning (alignment mismatch, bead-height measurement) rather than laser-generated ultrasound for volumetric flaw detection; no named pipeline-operator customer or performance metrics (POD, speed) disclosed.
locator: Article body, development-stage and capabilities sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Adjacent (not directly competing) US laser-inspection incumbent with deep pipeline-industry relationships; a credible pivot/fast-follow risk to F-10's laser-ultrasonic volumetric approach, distinct from Evident's contact-PAUT baseline.

india_origin_audit.notes: EWI is headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, USA. No India connection.

title: Magnetic Pulse Systems from Bmax Bring Innovative Capabilities to Metal Forming
authors_or_org: Magnetics Magazine, reporting on Bmax (iPulse Group)
claim_supported: Bmax's published magnetic-pulse-welding coil life: welding coils hold 30,000-100,000 cycles; forming/crimping coils hold 100,000-1,000,000 cycles depending on application; magnetic-pulse-crimped cable terminations are described as already the product of choice among heavy EV truck manufacturers for durability. Bmax serves automotive, aerospace, and packaging industries.
locator: Article body, coil-life and application paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: CRITICAL red-team finding for F-11: an established EU MPW competitor's own published coil-life specs (30,000-1,000,000 cycles) already exceed F-11's binding gate of >=10,000 shots by a factor of 3-100x, materially undermining the seed's framing of coil life as an unsolved blocker/core technical differentiator.

india_origin_audit.notes: US magnetics-industry trade publication reporting on a French/EU (iPulse Group) magnetic-pulse-welding equipment maker. No India connection.

title: Danfoss Editron Obtains Marine Certification in China
claim_supported: Danfoss Editron obtained final China Classification Society (CCS) type approval for its synchronous-reluctance-assisted permanent-magnet motors and frequency converters on 22 June 2020; partners with CSSC Shanghai Marine Diesel Engine Research Institute supplying a DC power system (two propulsion motors/inverters, bidirectional DC/DC converters, microgrid controllers) for Chinese electric vessels; manufactures in Nanjing, China.
locator: Press release body, certification-date and CSSC-partnership paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: Confirms an established Western competitor already holds CCS type approval (since 2020, not a new entrant) and has a CSSC-affiliated partnership; narrows F-12's 'no specialist supply base' framing for the marine DC-electrification stack, though Danfoss's certified scope (motors/converters) is adjacent to, not identical with, F-12's battery-protection/IMD product.

india_origin_audit.notes: Danfoss A/S is headquartered in Nordborg, Denmark. No India connection.

authors_or_org: China Classification Society (中国船级社, CCS)
claim_supported: China Classification Society published the 'Pure Battery-Powered Ship Technology and Inspection Interim Rules 2025' on 21 February 2025, a companion regulatory instrument to the 2025-edition Rules for Battery-Powered Ships (L10-045), confirming a broader, multi-instrument 2025 regulatory push for battery-powered vessels.

title: U.S. Imposes Entity List Restrictions on Large Chinese Semiconductor, Shipbuilding and Drone Companies
claim_supported: The US Commerce Department (BIS) added China State Shipbuilding Corporation (CSSC) to the Entity List on 18 December 2020, as part of a broader action against Chinese semiconductor, shipbuilding, and drone companies.
locator: Article body, date/company-name paragraph, fetched directly this session
notes: CRITICAL export-control finding for F-12's JV/licensing go-to-market plan: CSSC, named in the seed's own competitor list, is confirmed BIS Entity-Listed since 2020; any CSSC-affiliated JV partner triggers export-license requirements on US-origin technology.

india_origin_audit.notes: Kelley Drye & Warren LLP is a US law firm headquartered in Washington, DC / New York. No India connection.

title: New BIS Rule Expands Export Restrictions to Entities 50% Owned by Parties on the Entity and Military End-User Lists
authors_or_org: Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP
claim_supported: Effective 29 September 2025, BIS's new rule extends Entity List export restrictions to any foreign affiliate 50%+ owned (directly or indirectly) by an Entity-Listed party, operating under strict liability; companies must screen partners' ownership chains for Entity-List exposure.
locator: Article body, effective-date and practical-implications sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Establishes the binding compliance mechanism (the '50% rule') by which any CSSC-majority-owned JV/licensing partner for F-12 would automatically inherit CSSC's BIS Entity List restrictions -- a new, previously unflagged pre-JV screening requirement.

institutions.name: Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP

india_origin_audit.notes: Skadden is a US law firm headquartered in New York. No India connection.

title: 航运脱碳驶入快车道，电池巨头集体入局探金电动船舶新赛道 (Shipping Decarbonization Enters the Fast Lane; Battery Giants Collectively Enter the Electric-Ship Track)
authors_or_org: Fujian Provincial Department of Industry and Information Technology (福建省工信厅)
claim_supported: China's electric-vessel market is projected to reach RMB 36.75 billion in 2026 (global market $4.8B 2025 to $18.3B 2032); CATL's marine battery subsidiary (est. 2017) had equipped ~900 vessels by end-2025 and is doubling its team to ~500 people in 2026; CALB (中创新航) launched a 'Zhiyuan' marine battery series already certified by CCS, DNV, ABS, RINA, and BV; BYD signed a strategic partnership with Norway's Corvus Energy for marine LFP batteries; a Sunwoda/EVE-Energy-branded 'SAIL' marine battery line powers two 3,000-ton methanol-hybrid container ships under construction in Qingyuan, Guangdong.
locator: Article body, market-size and named-company paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: Provincial government source confirming both real, accelerating CN electric-vessel demand and a much more competitively crowded, multi-classification-certified competitor landscape than F-12's seed assumed -- CALB in particular already holds the DNV/ABS certification F-12 frames as its own export-hedge differentiator.

institutions.name: Fujian Provincial Department of Industry and Information Technology

india_origin_audit.notes: Official Chinese provincial government domain (.gov.cn). No India connection.
